justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Prototype pollution identified by name as first critical finding, (2) __proto__ key attack vector explicitly explained with payload example, (3) Global Object.prototype impact clearly stated — 'polluting Object.prototype globally. Every object in the process inherits isAdmin: true', (4) Safe merge fix recommended with specific key exclusions plus schema validation alternative, (5) Specific attack payload provided with curl reproduction command, (6) safe_to_deploy: false.

observations.text: Textbook-quality prototype pollution explanation with exact curl reproduction command
Correctly identified the privilege escalation impact (isAdmin example matches expected behavior)
Fix recommendations are specific and layered: key blacklist, schema validation with zod/joi, and replacing deepMerge entirely
Caught additional real bugs beyond the primary vulnerability: mass assignment, falsy value corruption, array handling, for-in inherited properties
Noted that pollution affects the entire Node process lifetime and persists until restart
Production readiness section correctly flags that corrupted DB records survive rollback

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: prototype pollution identified by name, __proto__ attack vector explained with specific payload ({"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}}), global Object.prototype impact explained, safe merge fix recommended (hasOwnProperty + key filtering, or schema validation), concrete curl reproduction provided, safe_to_deploy false.

observations.text: Prototype pollution identified as Critical with precise mechanism: for...in without hasOwnProperty and without __proto__/constructor/prototype filtering
Exact attack payload provided with curl reproduction command
Global impact clearly stated: 'every object in the Node process inherits the injected properties'
Multiple fix approaches: key filtering, schema validation with zod, or replacing deepMerge with spread
Goes beyond minimum by identifying mass assignment, array corruption, and defaults mutation issues
Rollback plan includes auditing database for __proto__ and unexpected fields

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: prototype pollution identified by name, __proto__ attack vector explained with specific payload, global Object.prototype impact explained, safe merge fix with key exclusion recommended plus schema validation alternative, specific curl attack example provided, safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Correctly identifies prototype pollution by name as Critical severity
Provides exact attack payload: {"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}} with curl reproduction
Clearly explains global Object.prototype contamination and privilege escalation impact
Fix recommends both key exclusion and schema validation as alternatives
Goes beyond requirements by identifying mass assignment and falsy-coercion bugs
Notes for...in inherited property iteration as a separate important issue
Rollback plan mentions process restart needed to clear polluted prototype
